title: leiophyllum buxifolium var. prostratum
intro: Leiophyllum buxifolium var. prostratum, also known as trailing rosymary or creeping rosymary, is a low-growing, prostrate shrub native to eastern North America. It is used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which aid in treating wounds, skin infections, and colds. The plant's essential oils have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. Leiophyllum buxifolium var. prostratum can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and improve mood.
